creator: wahjava@gmail.com
text:
,--[ On Sat, Jun 28, 2008 at 11:00:23AM +0200, Henrik Brix Andersen wrote:
| Hi,

Hi,

| Did you submit this patch upstream? I can't seem to find it at
| http://bugs.bitlbee.org/bitlbee/report/1

Submitted at http://bugs.bitlbee.org/bitlbee/ticket/421 .

| The patch looks a bit broken to me: $i will never be set if $arch =
| FreeBSD, but it is still referenced.

Following is an excerpt from patch:

+-for i in /lib /usr/lib /usr/local/lib; do
+-      if [ -f $i/libresolv.a ]; then
++if [ $arch = FreeBSD ]; then
++      if [ `strings /lib/libc.so.7 |grep res_query |wc -l` -gt 0 ];
then
+               echo '#define HAVE_RESOLV_A' >> config.h
+-              echo 'EFLAGS+='$i'/libresolv.a' >> Makefile.settings
+-              break
+-      fi
+-done
++      fi;
++else
++      for i in /lib /usr/lib /usr/local/lib; do
++              if [ -f $i/libresolv.a ]; then
++                      echo '#define HAVE_RESOLV_A' >> config.h
++                      echo 'EFLAGS+='$i'/libresolv.a' >>
Makefile.settings
++                      break
++              fi
++      done
++fi;

The above patch, tries to check for $arch = FreeBSD, if found checks for
'res_query' string in libc.so.7, and if that is found defines HAVE_RESOLV_A
in config.h. If $arch = FreeBSD but libc.so.7 doesn't contain 'res_query', then
it is not defining HAVE_RESOLV_A. If there is better way to check for
resolver routines, please replace this with those that way :) .

HTH
--
